body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58314 (commit 54ef6f748) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58314 added several significant features and breaking changes to the AgentServer Responses library: - `x-agent-session-id` response header is now returned on **all** Responses protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8 - Deterministic SHA-256-based session ID derivation (matching Python SDK: `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` → 63-char hex) - `ResponsesActivitySource` and `ResponsesTracingConstants` were made **internal** (breaking change for users who referenced them for custom spans) - Startup configuration logging via `ResponsesStartupLogger` (storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, event stream TTL) - Chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints - Isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) on all endpoint logs (values are never logged) - Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs ## Gaps Found - The README does not document the `x-agent-session-id` response header that clients will now receive on every protocol endpoint — users building client code against this server need to know to expect and optionally consume this header - The README troubleshooting/logging section still implies `ResponsesActivitySource` is publicly usable ("emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source") but does not note it is now internal — any handler author who was referencing this type directly will hit a compile error with no guidance - The deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`) is not documented in the README; this is important for operators who need to understand how session affinity works across requests - The startup configuration logging (what the server logs at startup, and which fields are masked for security) is absent from the README troubleshooting section - Chat isolation key enforcement behavior (mismatched key returns 404 indistinguishable from not-found) is not documented in the README Key Concepts or Troubleshooting sections
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit **1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` response header to Key Concepts** After the "### Thread safety" subsection, add a new subsection: ```markdown ### Session ID Response Header Every Responses protocol endpoint — POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — includes an `x-agent-session-id` response header per the agent server specification §8. The value is the resolved session ID for the request, derived in the following priority order: 1. `agent_session_id` from the POST request body 2. `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable 3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` (63 lowercase hex chars), where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` 4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available Clients that need to maintain session affinity across a multi-turn conversation can read this header from the POST response and supply it as `agent_session_id` in subsequent requests. ``` **1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section** Replace: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues. ``` With: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry distributed traces using the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source name. The `ResponsesActivitySource` class is internal to the framework; do not reference it directly in handler code. **Startup logging:** When the host starts, `ResponsesStartupLogger` logs the active configuration at `Information` level: - Storage provider type (e.g., `FoundryStorageProvider` or `InMemoryResponsesProvider`) - Default model - Default fetch history count - Event stream TTL **Endpoint diagnostic logging:** All Responses endpoints log at `Information` level with response ID, conversation ID, isolation key presence (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey` — key values are never logged), and final status. 4xx/5xx responses from inbound request logging are at `Warning` level. **Foundry storage logs:** Outbound Foundry storage API calls are logged with method, masked URI (everything before `/storage` is redacted; query parameters are stripped except `api-version`), status code, and correlation headers. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to surface these messages. ``` **1c. Add isolation key enforcement to Troubleshooting / Common errors** After the existing 404 Not Found bullet, add: ```markdown - **404 Not Found** (isolation mismatch): If a response was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns 404 intentionally — this is indistinguishable from not-found to protect tenant isolation. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this documentation update — the changes are prose-only additions to existing sections. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The additions above slot into existing subsections. No structural changes are needed. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — verify the README diff shows only the prose additions above (no snippet regions should have changed) 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Responses README missing x-agent-session-id header, internal activity source, and new observability features
